summary: Created Skills/careerspan-jd-intake/ skill with process_jd.py script that handles [JD] email intake flow

artifacts.description: Skill documentation with usage, arguments, and architecture
Main entry point - extracts JD, generates Hiring POV, outputs orchestrator instructions

capabilities_delivered: Extract company/role/requirements from JD text via LLM semantic analysis
Detect which of 5 Core Questions are answered vs missing
Output structured orchestrator_instructions for Airtable record creation, Drive upload, and email to Shivam
Support for PDF attachment text extraction
Dry-run mode for testing without side effects

architecture_decisions.decision: Script outputs orchestrator_instructions JSON rather than calling APIs directly
architecture_decisions.rationale: Keeps the script deterministic and testable. The pipeline orchestrator (or Zo heartbeat agent) handles actual tool calls. This separation makes debugging easier.

architecture_decisions.decision: LLM for all semantic extraction, no regex on meaningful content
architecture_decisions.rationale: Follows existing careerspan-candidate-guide pattern. JD parsing requires understanding context, not pattern matching.

architecture_decisions.decision: Config embedded in script rather than reading from central config.yaml
architecture_decisions.rationale: Reduces runtime dependencies. The values (Airtable IDs, Drive folder ID) are stable. If they change, update both places.

notes: The orchestrator_instructions output includes exact Zo tool names and field mappings
Email template uses {{DRIVE_LINK}} placeholder that orchestrator must replace
Permitted senders list currently only includes shivam@corridorx.io
Activity Log table logging included in orchestrator instructions (step 6)
lessons_for_ledger: When calling /zo/ask for structured JSON output, avoid output_format schema - instead prompt for JSON-only response and parse with fallback regex
Field names in Airtable have specific formatting (e.g., 'Salary Range (Checkbox)') - always check SCHEMA_TO_ADD.md for exact names
